title: Worktree adapter: bober worktree run + runInWorktree helper + MCP variant
description: Add the worktree-aware execution path that lets the cockpit launch multiple parallel runs on the same project without conflicts. Ships three pieces: (a) src/orchestrator/worktree.ts with runInWorktree(task, projectRoot, config, opts) — creates a git worktree, runs the pipeline inside it, cleans up per policy; (b) src/cli/commands/worktree.ts adding `bober worktree run <task> [--allow-dirty] [--keep-on-success]` CLI subcommand; (c) src/mcp/tools/run-in-worktree.ts registering bober_run_in_worktree({ task, allowDirty?, keepOnSuccess? }) MCP tool. RunState (Sprint 1) gains worktreePath and branch fields so bober_get_run_status surfaces them.

successCriteria.description: src/orchestrator/worktree.ts exports runInWorktree(task, projectRoot, config, opts) which creates a git worktree under .bober/worktrees/<runId> (overridable via pipeline.worktreeRoot config) and runs runPipeline inside it.

Branch name derives from generator.branchPattern config (default 'bober/<slug>'); created via `git worktree add <path> -b <branch>`; baseline branch defaults to current HEAD or 'main' if HEAD is detached.

On pipeline success, the worktree is removed via `git worktree remove <path>` when pipeline.cleanupWorktreeOnSuccess !== false (default true) AND the --keep-on-success flag was NOT passed. On pipeline failure, the worktree is always retained and its path is printed to stderr.

Dirty working tree rejected with exit code 1 and a clear error message identifying the dirty files, unless --allow-dirty (CLI) or allowDirty=true (MCP) is passed. Mirrors `git worktree add`'s default safety.
RunState gains optional fields worktreePath and branch; both are populated by runInWorktree before kicking off the pipeline and persisted via the Sprint 1 atomic-write helper.
`bober worktree run <task>` CLI subcommand prints `{ runId, branch, worktreePath, projectRoot }` JSON to stdout on completion; non-zero exit code on pipeline failure.

bober_run_in_worktree MCP tool registered, returns `{ runId, branch, worktreePath, status: 'running' }` immediately (fire-and-forget like bober_run), and reflects the worktree fields in bober_get_run_status output.
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, and `npm run test` all pass; existing tests stay green.

nonGoals: Do not introduce a new git library (e.g. isomorphic-git, simple-git) — shell out via the existing src/utils/git.ts helpers; extend git.ts only as needed.
Do not auto-merge the worktree branch back into the base branch on success — leave that decision to the cockpit / user.
Do not implement worktree pruning or garbage collection of orphaned worktrees in this sprint — document it as a follow-up.
Do not change the existing bober_run tool to run in a worktree by default — bober_run_in_worktree is a new opt-in tool.
Do not introduce new npm dependencies.
stopConditions: All required success criteria pass evaluation.

Integration smoke: run `bober worktree run "trivial task"` against the agent-bober repo itself; verify the worktree was created at .bober/worktrees/<runId>, the branch matches the configured pattern, and the worktree is cleaned up on success.
Manual dirty-tree test: modify a tracked file, run `bober worktree run "x"`, verify exit code 1 and dirty-files error message.
definitionOfDone: A cockpit operator can launch arbitrarily many parallel bober runs on the same project, each isolated in its own git worktree on its own branch, without conflicts. The worktree lifecycle is safe (refuses dirty trees by default; retains on failure for debugging) and observable (worktreePath and branch are persisted to RunState and exposed via bober_get_run_status). The CLI subcommand and MCP tool both follow existing patterns and reuse the runInWorktree helper.
assumptions: git CLI is available on the host; this is a soft prereq of agent-bober already (existing src/utils/git.ts assumes it). No new prereq introduced.

Worktree slug derivation: take the first 60 chars of the task description, lowercase, replace non-alphanumeric with '-', strip leading/trailing dashes; matches the existing generator.branchPattern '{feature-name}' substitution at src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts (review existing branch-name derivation and reuse).
Detached-HEAD baseline: if the current HEAD is detached, fall back to 'main' (or 'master' if main is absent). Document the fallback in stderr.

Worktrees nested inside .bober/worktrees/ inside the project root are git-safe because git ignores .bober/ by default (verify via existing .gitignore). If the project does NOT gitignore .bober/, the worktree creation still works but may surprise users — document in stderr.
outOfScope: Garbage collection of orphaned worktrees from prior failed runs (documented follow-up).
Worktree-aware bober_status (the cockpit uses bober_get_run_status by runId instead).
generatorNotes: PATTERN TO FOLLOW: src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:107-122 buildProjectContext shows the existing src/utils/git.ts import surface (getCurrentBranch). Extend src/utils/git.ts with addWorktree(path, branch, baseBranch?), removeWorktree(path, force?), isClean() helpers — each shells out via child_process.exec to git CLI. Use Promise<{ stdout, stderr }> conventions. PATTERN: src/cli/commands/incident.ts and src/cli/commands/playbook.ts show the commander subcommand registration pattern (registerIncidentCommand etc.). For bober_run_in_worktree MCP tool: copy src/mcp/tools/run.ts (registerRunTool) as the template — same fire-and-forget pattern, same McpError vs JSON-soft-error convention. The runId for the worktree path must be generated BEFORE the worktree is created so the path is deterministic; pass that runId into RunManager.startRun via a new opts parameter (extend startRun's signature to accept opts: { runId?, worktreePath?, branch? }; preserve back-compat by treating those as optional). On pipeline completion callback in RunManager, run the cleanup logic (rm worktree if success && cleanup enabled). For schema: add pipeline.worktreeRoot and pipeline.cleanupWorktreeOnSuccess to src/config/schema.ts as optional fields (default '.bober/worktrees' and true respectively). For the dirty-tree check: `git status --porcelain` and assert empty output; if non-empty, list the dirty files in the error message.
evaluatorNotes: Verify runInWorktree actually shells out to git CLI (not a stub). Verify the worktree is created at the expected path, the branch is created with the expected name, and pipeline.runPipeline is invoked with the worktree path as projectRoot (not the original projectRoot — the pipeline must operate inside the worktree). Verify cleanup-on-success uses `git worktree remove` (not rm -rf, which would leave dangling git metadata). Verify dirty-tree rejection works by writing a dirty file in a test fixture repo. Verify the worktreePath and branch fields appear in bober_get_run_status output. Verify pipeline.runPipeline running INSIDE the worktree means commits land on the worktree's branch, not the parent's branch (this is a critical correctness check — exercise it with a small fixture repo). Negative test: starting two worktree runs concurrently both succeed without conflict (each gets its own subdirectory).
